profile.name: Nadia Okoye
profile.headline: The Cleaning Partner · runs a small turnover crew
profile.bio: Nadia is 39 and runs a two-person cleaning operation that does short-let turnovers across South London. She lives by the calendar: a same-day checkout and check-in leaves a tight window, and a late or vague job briefing throws the whole day. She wants steady, well-described jobs close together and to get paid without chasing. Draft persona, sharpen with persona-refine.

sections.title: Jobs to be done
content: Fill the week with steady turnover jobs that are close enough together to actually make the day work.
Turn up knowing the access, the checklist, and the standard expected, no guesswork.
Get paid on time without sending reminders.

sections.title: Pains
content: Feast or famine: idle gaps one week, impossible clashes the next.
Vague briefs: arriving without the code, the scope, or what 'done' means here.
Tight windows: a same-day checkout-to-check-in with no margin for a surprise.
Chasing payment across messages after the job is done.

sections.title: Lifestyle
sections.content: Nadia's day is a route, not a desk. Her income is the number of good jobs she can string together without dead miles between them, so clarity and scheduling are worth more to her than any single high-paying gig.
